title: How are evangelions created
wikitext:
[]: How are evangelions created?             Quote Post by Cypher » Thu Jul 09, 2009 8:23 am I know they are clones of Adam/Lilith but does that mean NERV grows them and then attaches the armor? or do they need to piece the eva's together after creating each part?

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Thu Jul 09, 2009 8:48 am They're grown. If NERV just kept dozens of spare arms and legs lying around for repairs or creating new units, we would have seen something. This is also why all of the defected units in the Eva Graveyard were dumped in various stages of completion but always have a core head and spinal column.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jul 09, 2009 8:54 am They grow them whole. Here is an image of Unit-01 being grown on Lilith. [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/Image:Tank_image_11a_bunshin.jpg[/url] This was originally one of the "tank images" from the original director's cut of ep. 23 (removed for Renewal/Platinum). It later showed up in the Full OP that was released with Renewal.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Thu Jul 09, 2009 9:27 am When two EVAs fall in love, they want to express this love....Physically.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Jul 09, 2009 11:53 am That is where Reichu comes in ^ Just picture looking up and seeing that..that is a lot of lovin.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Jul 09, 2009 12:29 pm It seems likely the process for growing 01 was unique, the only one grown from an intact SoL. Adamic Evas seem still to still be grown, but obviously not from an intact SoL and apparently not as a unit (skull/spine or head/torso seems to be the "core" that is developed first with appendages further grown later, based on the various graveyard views of the skeletal remains)...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 09, 2009 1:51 pm VoidEater wrote: It seems likely the process for growing 01 was unique, the only one grown from an intact SoL. Adamic Evas seem still to still be grown, but obviously not from an intact SoL and apparently not as a unit (skull/spine or head/torso seems to be the "core" that is developed first with appendages further grown later, based on the various graveyard views of the skeletal remains)...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 09, 2009 2:42 pm Do Evas even undergo rot? The idea that Evas are put together like Mrs. Potato Heads or something always struck me as a bit... dysfunctional. I mean, both Adam and the Angels indicate that embryological development is the norm for these beings, so what's with the seeming suggestion that Gehirn/Nerv would grow Evas in pieces and then somehow combine them into a functional body? A biological body is a ridiculously complicated "piece of machinery", and trying to slap one together from "spare parts" would involve light-years of extra work... And how are you going to be able to grow specific Eva body parts with any kind of reliability (especially the kind of reliability required for "assembly" into a single coherent body) if you haven't mastered the ability of growing entire embryos? ...And if you've mastered the ability of growing entire embryos, why not just do that from the start? A possibility, anyway, is that they intentionally just grew CNSes (and not much more) at the beginning for experimental research, and gradually allowed more of the Evas' bodies to develop (which costs more money, of course) as they got a better idea of the directions they needed to head in. Additionally, we have no way of knowing what was done to the prototypes before they were dumped, so for all we know some of them might have had limbs. (In the OA version of the graveyard scene, some of the Evas do have arms, and the "chalk outlines" leave spaces for legs. So who knows, really...) Of course, this could well just be wishful thinking on my part.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Jul 09, 2009 4:35 pm ^ That makes more sense. While I'm not sure Eva development necessarily needs to follow Adamic embryo growth, I'm not happy with the Mr. Potato Head thing either. Pointless meandering: For some reason I had this notion of them growing head/spine as an early form, then triggering more pieces as they got better. Maybe because of the Rei-legs or whatever they are on Lilith (though a special case) or the way Rei's body seemed to start sprouting things when invaded or the Production Series could sprout things...IIRC... If there were always entire bodies in the prototyping, I would think the skulls would eventually fall away from the separated vertabra in the same way the presumed limbs fell away, in at least some cases. The image of the more-parts prototype begins to enter my mind, again suggesting stages of growth. Image: tadpole Eva. Then it becomes a frog. Maybe following human embrylogical development, but at a larger scale (though not matching Adamic development in scale).

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Thu Jul 09, 2009 4:50 pm If you have seen the cloning sequence from Star Wars Episode II, then you have half of the picture right there. You would just need some Adam or Lilith DNA, replicate it, put it in a jar, fill it with blue liquid of unknown origin, and let it sit for a while. Once the organic part of the Eva is complete, you cover it in some metal plates (for armor), carve a hole in it's spine (for the entry plug) and then add paint and other accessories, such as shoulder pylons or horns. Once that is done, the Eva only needs a soul from a willing volunteer to function. Once you have the soul, slay a virgin girl and make a pentagram in her blood, then recite random words in ominous Latin. If you can do all of this then your Eva will be complete!

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Jul 09, 2009 5:01 pm ^Please market a kit, I'll clear out the garage...mm...make that the block...

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Jul 09, 2009 5:05 pm ~My Friend God Creation Kit~ Comes with everything you need to turn the world to tang.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Thu Jul 09, 2009 7:00 pm ^Perfect tagline!

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Thu Jul 09, 2009 10:02 pm thewayneiac wrote: They grow them whole. Here is an image of Unit-01 being grown on Lilith. [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/Image:Tank_image_11a_bunshin.jpg[/url] This was originally one of the "tank images" from the original director's cut of ep. 23 (removed for Renewal/Platinum). It later showed up in the Full OP that was released with Renewal.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jul 09, 2009 10:28 pm I Accidentally Dracula wrote: I'm scratching my head on who is supposed to be what in that picture.

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Thu Jul 09, 2009 10:51 pm I was able to discern that much, mainly due to the position of the cross. Beyond that, though, all I see are two undetailed mannequin torsos lying in spaghetti.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri Jul 10, 2009 11:09 am ^This process is, apparently, unique to 01 and should not be seen as how the other Evas were created. 01 is the only Eva made from Lilith. NERV didn't have a whole Adam after the Katsuragi expedition. A random thread that might spark stuff: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3083&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=0

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Jul 11, 2009 12:08 pm VoidEater wrote: I'm not happy with the Mr. Potato Head thing either.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Jul 30, 2009 12:57 am VoidEater wrote: ^This process is, apparently, unique to 01 and should not be seen as how the other Evas were created. 01 is the only Eva made from Lilith. NERV didn't have a whole Adam after the Katsuragi expedition. A random thread that might spark stuff: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3083&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=0

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jul 30, 2009 1:11 am Electric Sachiel wrote: I was always under the impression that they regrew new limbs and reattached them to the Evas.
